messages.text: At Belmont, moonlight softened the garden. Lorenzo and Jessica spoke of famous lovers while music filled the house with harmony.

messages.name: Lorenzo
messages.text: How sweet the moonlight sleeps upon this bank. Sit, Jessica, and listen.

messages.name: Jessica
messages.text: I am never merry when I hear sweet music. Perhaps it makes the soul too honest.
Portia and Nerissa arrived before their husbands and entered as if they had never left. Soon Bassanio, Gratiano, and Antonio followed.

messages.name: Gratiano
messages.text: Nerissa, do not quarrel before I greet you. I gave your ring to a clerk, but for a worthy reason.

messages.name: Nerissa
messages.text: A clerk? Then let that clerk share your bed, since he has my token.
Bassanio tried to look innocent, but Portia noticed the empty finger at once. The small ring now commanded the room.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Where is the ring I gave you, my lord?

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: If you knew the man to whom I gave it, and why, you would forgive me.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: If you had known the value of the token, you would not have parted with it.
The quarrel grew comic but sharp. Bassanio had to confess fully, blaming gratitude, honor, and Antonio in equal measure.

messages.name: Antonio
messages.text: I am the unhappy cause. Once I pledged my body for him; now I pledge my soul that he will never break faith again.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Then you shall be his surety again. Give him this ring and tell him to keep it better.
Bassanio stared. It was the same ring. Nerissa produced Gratiano's ring too, and laughter began to restore the room.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: I was the doctor, and Nerissa was the clerk. We have a document from Bellario to prove it.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: You saved Antonio and then judged me at home. I deserve both thanks and shame.
More news followed. A letter announced that three of Antonio's ships had safely come to harbor after all. His fortune was restored.

messages.name: Antonio
messages.text: Sweet lady, you give life and livelihood in the same night.
Nerissa gave Jessica and Lorenzo a deed naming each one an heir to Shylock. The inheritance carried relief and unease together.
Belmont ended the quarrel with music, but every restored fortune had passed through risk, law, and confession first.
The music in Belmont did not erase Venice; it answered it. After the noise of court, harmony made each guilty pause easier to hear.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: You gave away my token to a doctor. I wonder what else gratitude persuaded you to give.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: Nothing but the ring, and even that I would restore with my right hand if I could.
When Portia revealed the document, Bassanio saw that the judge who saved Antonio had also watched his own failure at close range.

messages.name: Antonio
messages.text: I was debtor once, then defendant, now surety for a marriage promise. Let this prove my faith better.
The announcement of restored ships widened the ending beyond romance. Fortune returned to Antonio, while Jessica and Lorenzo became heir to a painful estate.
Forgiveness in Belmont therefore sounded like music with a minor note: bright enough to dance to, honest enough to remember the cost.
Portia did not humiliate Bassanio merely to win. She restored the ring so the promise could begin again with clearer knowledge.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: Your mercy after my confession teaches me more than your anger did.
The reveal gave Portia authority in her own house without the robe. She no longer needed disguise because the proof had arrived before argument.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Keep the ring now with knowledge, not with easy poetry.
Bassanio accepted the rebuke because love had survived being corrected.
